title: Multi-Turn Conversation Dataset Schema
description: JSON schema describing the structure and requirements for multi-turn conversation datasets in the MLPerf Inference Endpoint Benchmarking System

basicMessageTypes.title: Basic Message Types
basicMessageTypes.description: Plain conversational messages without tool calls

system.description: System prompt (typically only on first user turn)

role.description: Message role - assistant responds to user

toolCallMessage.title: Assistant Message with Tool Calls
toolCallMessage.description: Assistant message that dispatches one or more tool calls. Role must be 'assistant' with a non-empty tool_calls array (OpenAI wire format).

role.description: Role for a tool-dispatching assistant message.

content.description: Optional textual prefix alongside tool dispatch (e.g., 'I will investigate this with bash'). Typically null/absent.

tool_calls.description: List of tool calls dispatched by the assistant

id.description: Unique identifier for this tool call (e.g., 'functions.bash:0')

name.description: Name of the tool/function to invoke

toolMessage.title: Tool Result Message
toolMessage.description: Tool execution results as a list. Single results have one entry; parallel results have multiple entries.

tool_results.description: List of tool execution results. Single tool calls have one entry; parallel tool calls have multiple entries.

tool_call_id.description: ID of the tool call this result corresponds to

content.description: Output/result content from the tool execution

generationParameters.title: Generation Parameters
generationParameters.description: Optional parameters controlling the model's behavior for generation

model.description: Model name override for this turn

max_new_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

max_completion_tokens.description: OpenAI API compatible max tokens parameter

stream.description: Whether to use streaming for this turn

temperature.description: Sampling temperature (0 = deterministic, higher = more random)

delay_seconds.description: Delay in seconds before issuing this client turn

tools.description: OpenAI tool definitions for tool-calling models

oneOf.title: Plain Conversation Row
oneOf.description: A single row representing a plain user or assistant message

oneOf.title: Tool Call Row
oneOf.description: A single row representing an assistant dispatch of tool calls

oneOf.title: Tool Result Row
oneOf.description: A single row representing one or more tool results

data.content: I need help resetting my password
data.system: You are a helpful customer support agent

data.content: I'd be happy to help. Can you provide your email address?

examples.title: Assistant with tool calls (converter/OpenAI wire format)

data.content: What's the best way to optimize this code?

documentation.overview: Multi-turn conversation datasets enable benchmarking of realistic conversational AI workloads where each turn depends on previous responses. The system maintains conversation history and enforces turn sequencing.

requiredFields.description: Unique identifier for each conversation. All rows belonging to the same conversation must share the same conversation_id.
Turn number within conversation (1-indexed). Must be consecutive starting at 1 (i.e., 1, 2, 3, …, N with no gaps or duplicates).
Speaker role. 'user' or 'tool' are client-initiated turns. 'assistant' is the server response — either a terminal reply or a tool dispatch (with tool_calls field).

Message content. Required for 'user' role and plain 'assistant' rows. For tool-dispatching assistant rows, content may be omitted (null/absent). For 'tool' rows using tool_results (merged parallel results), top-level content is absent — results are in the tool_results array instead.

optionalFields.description: System prompt (typically only on first user turn). Applied to all messages in the conversation.
Tool calls dispatched by assistant (for tool-dispatching 'assistant' rows). Each element has {id, type, function: {name, arguments}}.
Tool execution results (required for all 'tool' role rows). Each element has {tool_call_id, content}. Single results have one entry; parallel results have multiple entries.

Optional. Inter-turn wall-clock delay (seconds) the client should sleep before issuing this row. Typically set only on 'tool' or 'user' rows. Honored at run time only when MultiTurnConfig.inject_tool_delay=True; otherwise ignored. Missing or zero means no sleep.
Model name override for this turn.
Maximum tokens to generate for this turn.

OpenAI tool definitions forwarded to the endpoint for tool-calling models. The converter attaches this only to client-turn rows (user and tool) to avoid duplicating the large array on every assistant row. Hand-authored datasets typically place it on the first user turn.

validRoleSequences.name: Plain conversation
validRoleSequences.description: Standard alternating conversation without tool use.

validRoleSequences.name: Agentic with tools
validRoleSequences.sequence: user → assistant → tool → [assistant → tool]* → assistant → user
validRoleSequences.description: Agent dispatches tools (assistant with tool_calls), executes them, and returns results before final response. 'tool → user' is also valid when no terminal assistant response is needed before the next user turn.

validationRules.rule: Turn numbers must be consecutive starting at 1
validationRules.violation: Turn sequence is not exactly 1, 2, 3, …, N (missing, duplicate, or out-of-range turns)

validationRules.rule: Role sequences must follow the state machine
validationRules.violation: Invalid transition (e.g., 'user' directly followed by 'user', consecutive 'assistant' rows). Note: 'tool → user' IS a valid transition. The state machine also implicitly enforces that the first row must be a user turn.

notValidated: tool_results[*].tool_call_id pairing: the validator does NOT verify that tool_call_id values inside tool_results items reference a prior assistant tool_calls entry. Correct pairing is the dataset author's or converter's responsibility.

fields.note: Expands to one OpenAI tool message per result entry at the wire layer

conversionFromSnapshot.description: Agentic datasets are often stored as full-conversation snapshots. Use scripts/convert_agentic_snapshot.py to convert.
conversionFromSnapshot.sourceFormat: Each JSONL line is a complete conversation snapshot with 'messages' array
conversionFromSnapshot.targetFormat: Each JSONL line is a single message row with conversation metadata
process: Extract conversation_id, conversation_idx, and messages array
Collapse consecutive user messages into a single user row (newline-joined content)
Emit all tool result rows as tool_results arrays (single results have one entry; consecutive tool results from parallel dispatch are merged into one row with multiple entries)
Flatten messages into individual rows, numbering turns sequentially from 1
Attach system prompt to first user row only
Attach tools array to client-turn rows (user and tool) only — not to assistant rows
Tool-dispatching assistant messages are written as role 'assistant' with tool_calls (OpenAI wire format)

performanceNotes: Pre-built messages: The system pre-computes complete message lists during dataset load() for efficient turn serving
Memory efficiency: ~1KB per turn average; 1000 conversations × 10 turns = ~10MB
Hot path: Only client turns (user/tool) are issued; assistant turns remain in backing store for history

commonErrors.cause: Violates state machine (e.g., user→user, user→tool, consecutive assistant rows)
commonErrors.fix: Verify alternation or use conversion script for agentic data

commonErrors.cause: Turn sequence has gaps, duplicates, or doesn't start at 1
commonErrors.fix: Ensure turns are numbered 1, 2, 3, …, N with no missing or duplicate values

commonErrors.cause: Previous turn took too long to complete
commonErrors.fix: Increase turn_timeout_s in configuration or check endpoint performance
